top_signals.title: China Bets on Ultra-Deep Shale Gas to Boost Energy Security
top_signals.summary: China’s state-owned oil and gas major and top refiner Sinopec is ramping up exploration in the shale formations of the Sichuan basin with a view to increasing the country’s shale gas production by a third over the next ten years. Currently, China’s shale gas total is below government targets, Reuters noted in a report on the news, adding shale gas represents only a tenth of China’s total natural gas production. Beijing has a shale gas output target of 80 to 100 billion cu m by 2030. This has prompted Sinopec and other state-owned…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Three Short Tales on War Brad Carson Wants You to Read
top_signals.summary:
Editor’s Note: This is a new occasional series brought to you by War on the Rocks. If you would like to pitch your own version, please refer to the contact information and guidance on our submissions page.Every war, it seems, produces its famous novel, a book that captures not merely the tactics and the terrain but the moral weather of its time. The American Civil War gave us The Red Badge of Courage. World War I gave us All Quiet on the Western Front. World War II offered more competitors, as if the scale of the catastrophe demanded more witnesses;

The post Three Short Tales on War Brad Carson Wants You to Read appeared first on War on the Rocks.

Brazil’s Grilling Tradition Challenged by Beef Prices
top_signals.summary: Soaring prices for the world’s largest beef producer, Brazil, mean households will be buying less red meat when they gather to watch Brazil’s first game of the FIFA World Cup this Saturday.
